body.title: Ground Loop: consolidate CC recovery into on_failure hook
body.result: cc_recovery handler implemented; stderr→log file; Igor scatter call commented pending consequence ticket; 6/6 tests
body.intention: I intend that all per-device 'failed to start → invoke CC with error' recovery paths live in one place (Ground Loop on_failure hook) so adding CC recovery to any new service costs zero code — just on_failure: cc_recovery in the plugin YAML.
body.description:
UU startup and Igor startup both have scattered 'if process fails → call claude --dangerously-skip-permissions with the error' recovery paths. Move them to a single Ground Loop on_failure handler so any plugin can opt in with one YAML line.

Implement the cc_recovery on_failure handler in Ground Loop: captures the failure reason + process stderr, invokes claude --dangerously-skip-permissions with a structured error prompt, logs the invocation.

Remove the scattered per-device CC recovery calls from UU and Igor startup once the Ground Loop handler is proven. Leave the old paths in place (commented) until T-consequence-ground-loop clears.

**Validation test (Akien's idea):** Break Igor on purpose (corrupt a config value or block its port), confirm Ground Loop detects the failure and invokes CC with the error report. Restore Igor. Test confirms the full recovery path end-to-end.

**Affected files:** devices/ground_loop/on_failure.py (new), unseen_university startup (cc recovery call moved), devices/igor/ startup error handler (cc recovery call moved)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; log every CC invocation with full error context
**Scope boundary:** In scope: on_failure cc_recovery handler; remove scattered per-device paths after validation. Out of scope: other on_failure handlers, non-CC recovery strategies.
**Completion criteria:** (1) on_failure: cc_recovery in any plugin YAML triggers CC invocation on repeated failure; (2) integration test: Igor deliberately broken → Ground Loop fires CC recovery → logged; (3) no duplicate CC recovery calls in UU/Igor startup; (4) all tests pass.
